title: Transaction Type
text: Transaction Type Transaction mode for all operations in the component when run in a distributed transaction processing environment. Specify the transaction type property as part of an associated list with the COMMIT ( Transaction Auto Commit ) property: TYPE= TypeNumber ; COMMIT=T | F Value Representation Description 1 Transaction Support A global transaction context is not mandatory for the component. 2 No Transaction Support The component cannot run without a transaction context. 3 Requires Running Transaction The component can only be used when there is a running transaction. If no transaction is available, the client starts a new transaction. 4 Requires Transaction The component requires a transaction. This means that if a transaction is not present, the component starts and commits its own transaction. 5 Requires New Transaction The component requires a new transaction. This means that the component cannot share the transaction context in which its instance was created. The component must then start and commit its own transaction. Description In a distributed transaction environment, the client can start a transaction, and one or more services started by the client can participate in the transaction. When an operation that is located on a server is called, the server changes the DBMS to this transaction, so the service works on a transaction that has already been started. Applies To Signature Related Links Transaction Auto Commit Determines whether transactions are committed if the component instance that started the transaction is deleted. Valid values are True or False (default). By default, transactions are rolled back if the component instance is deleted. Use Transactions Determines whether the use of transactions is enabled for the component when run in a distributed transaction processing environment. Valid values are True or False (default).
